articles.title: Bengaluru braces for four days of rain as Karnataka faces rising heatwave threat - NewsFirst Prime
articles.full_text:
Thunderstorms, lightning and strong winds likely in Bengaluru; weather department warns of sharp temperature rise in several north Karnataka districts
Bengaluru is likely to experience moderate rainfall accompanied by thunder, lightning and gusty winds over the next four days, according to the latest weather forecast issued by authorities.
The weather department has advised citizens to remain alert, especially during evening hours, as sudden rain activity and strong winds may affect normal movement in several parts of the city.
Officials said Bengaluru may receive moderate rainfall from today onwards, with changing weather conditions expected to continue for the next few days. Residents have been urged to take precautionary measures during periods of lightning and thunderstorms.
At the same time, the weather department has also issued a separate warning for several districts in Karnataka regarding a significant rise in temperatures over the next five days.
According to the forecast, districts in north interior Karnataka are expected to record temperatures above normal levels. The districts likely to be affected include Belagavi, Bagalkote, Vijayapura, Koppal, Raichur, Kalaburagi, Bidar and Yadgir.

Authorities have warned that some places could experience heatwave-like conditions due to the continuous rise in daytime temperatures.
In view of the weather changes across the state, officials have issued several precautionary guidelines for the public.
People have been advised to drink plenty of water regularly, even if they do not feel thirsty. Authorities suggested consuming fluids such as water, tender coconut water and buttermilk to keep the body hydrated.
The public has also been advised to avoid unnecessary travel between 12 pm and 4 pm, when heat intensity is expected to be at its peak in many districts.
Health experts and officials recommended wearing light-coloured, loose-fitting cotton clothes to stay comfortable during hot weather conditions. Citizens stepping outdoors have also been advised to use umbrellas, caps or other protective items to avoid direct exposure to sunlight.

Special care has been advised for children and elderly people, as extreme heat can affect them more quickly. Families have been asked to closely monitor their health and ensure they remain hydrated.
Authorities also suggested avoiding oily and heavily spicy food during the heat period and instead consuming more fruits and vegetables.
Officials further warned that people experiencing symptoms such as headaches, dizziness or unusual tiredness should immediately consult a doctor.
